LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FRATERNITY OF DELTA SIGMA PI, INC., DELTA SIGMA PI-GAMMA OMICRON'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (CCP 1281.2) AND TO STAY ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION (CCP 1281.2). DEFENDANTS INTERNATIONAL FRATERNITY OF DELTA SIGMA PI, INC. AND GAMMA OMICRON'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE CIRCUMSTANCES SURROUNDING PLAINTIFF'S SIGNING OF THE MEMBERSHIP AGREEMENT DO NOT EXHIBIT PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY. WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS YOUNG AND NOT LEGALLY TRAINED, THE AGREEMENT WAS A SINGLE PAGE AND PLAINTIFF HAD SIX DAYS TO REVIEW IT AND ASK QUESTIONS. JOINING A FRATERNAL ORGANIZATION IS ENTIRELY VOLUNTARY AND NOT ESSENTIAL.NOR IS THE ARBITRATION PROVISION SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE CHOICE OF LAW CLAUSE DOES NOT PRESENT THE SAME HARDSHIPS AS A FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE. PLAINTIFF CITES NO CASE FOR THE NOTION THAT THE BREADTH OF CLAIMS COVERED BY AN ARBITRATION PROVISION SUPPORTS A FINDING OF UNCONSCIONABILITY. IN ANY EVENT, THE PROVISION SPECIFICALLY INCLUDES PERSONAL INJURY CLAIMS. THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE'S PLAIN LANGUAGE SUPPORTS DEFENDANTS' REPRESENTATION OF MUTUALITY, AND DEFENDANTS SEEK TO BRING ALL PARTIES INTO ARBITRATION. (SEE CCP 1281.2(C).)COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2) And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2)
Off Calendar Nov-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FRATERNITY OF DELTA SIGMA PI, INC., DELTA SIGMA PI-GAMMA OMICRON's Petition To Compel Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2) And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2).

Defendants International Fraternity of Delta Sigma Pi, Inc. and Gamma Omicron's petition to compel arbitration and to stay action pending arbitration is GRANTED. The circumstances surrounding plaintiff's signing of the membership agreement do not exhibit procedural unconscionability. While plaintiff was young and not legally trained, the agreement was a single page and plaintiff had six days to review it and ask questions. Joining a fraternal organization is entirely voluntary and not essential.

Nor is the arbitration provision substantively unconscionable. The choice of law clause does not present the same hardships as a forum selection clause. Plaintiff cites no case for the notion that the breadth of claims covered by an arbitration provision supports a finding of unconscionability. In any event, the provision specifically includes personal injury claims. The arbitration clause's plain language supports defendants' representation of mutuality, and defendants seek to bring all parties into arbitration. (See CCP 1281.2(c).)

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
